explanation: H isotopes: Protium (¹H, no neutron), Deuterium (²H or D, 1 neutron), Tritium (³H or T, 2 neutrons). Tritium is radioactive.
name: Hydrogen Isotopes

text: The industrial preparation of hydrogen by steam reforming of methane gives:
explanation: Steam reforming: \( \text{CH}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\text{Ni, 1270K}} \text{CO} + 3\text{H}_2 \) (synthesis gas/water gas). Most H₂ is produced this way.
name: Steam Reforming

text: Heavy water (D₂O) differs from ordinary water in having:
options: Higher density and higher boiling point
Lower density and lower boiling point
explanation: D₂O: density 1.11 g/mL (vs 1.00 for H₂O), bp 101.4°C (vs 100°C), mp 3.8°C (vs 0°C). Heavier due to deuterium.
name: Heavy Water Properties

explanation: H₂O₂ has non-planar open book structure. Dihedral angle: 111.5° in gas phase. H–O–O bond angle: 96.87°.
name: Structure of H₂O₂

text: H₂O₂ acts as both oxidising and reducing agent. As reducing agent it is oxidised to:
explanation: H₂O₂ as reducing agent: \( 2\text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \to 2\text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{O}_2 \). It donates electrons (loses O, producing O₂).
name: H₂O₂ as Reducing Agent

text: The concentration of H₂O₂ solution is expressed as '20 volume'. This means:
options: 1 L of H₂O₂ gives 20 L of O₂ at STP
explanation: Volume strength: 1 L of 20-volume H₂O₂ gives 20 L of O₂ at STP. Molarity = Volume strength / 11.2.
name: Volume Strength of H₂O₂

explanation: Ionic (saline) hydrides: formed by active metals (Group 1, 2). e.g., NaH, CaH₂. H exists as H⁻ (hydride ion).
name: Ionic Hydrides

text: In the water gas shift reaction: \( \text{CO} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightleftharpoons \)
explanation: Water gas shift reaction: \( \text{CO} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightleftharpoons \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2 \). Used to increase H₂ yield.
name: Water Gas Shift Reaction

text: The bleaching action of H₂O₂ is due to:
explanation: H₂O₂ bleaches by oxidation: releases nascent oxygen [O] which oxidises coloured organic matter. Used for hair bleaching, disinfection.
name: Bleaching by H₂O₂

text: Hard water contains dissolved salts of:
explanation: Hard water: contains Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ ions (as bicarbonates, sulfates, chlorides). Temporary hardness (bicarbonates) removed by boiling.
name: Hard Water

text: The oxidation state of oxygen in H₂O₂ is:
explanation: In H₂O₂: H is +1, so O = -1. (In H₂O: O = -2; in O₂: O = 0; in F₂O: O = +2)
name: Oxidation State in H₂O₂

text: Which is the most abundant element in the universe?
explanation: Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe (~75% by mass). It is the fuel for stars via nuclear fusion.
name: Most Abundant in Universe

text: The reaction of active metals with water producing H₂:
name: Na and Water Reaction

text: Hydrides of Group 16 elements boiling points follow the order:
explanation: H₂O has anomalously high bp (100°C) due to H-bonding. Without H-bonding, bp trend would be H₂Te > H₂Se > H₂S > H₂O.
name: Boiling Point of Group 16 Hydrides

text: The enthalpy of formation of water (\(\Delta H^\circ_f\)) at 25°C is approximately:
name: Enthalpy of Formation of Water
